column1: C[druuchi: HI, have fun and a good game.
labrys: yep, thanks for the game :)
LevelOneDev: I have no idea what this move accomplished but I was attempting to separate both groups and keep access to outside I feel like it isn't really a good move.
C[labrys: you trying the 9x9 tournament starting in a minute?
hurik: i won't have much time the next day, so i will only make occasionally move the next three days
labrys: no worries. tournament has started now, so I'll have to disappear for a bit
hurik: will check tommorrow how you was doing :)
C[hurik: how was your tournament yesterday?
C[labrys: won one because the player didn't show up, then lost shamefully in the next round
C[hurik: so what are you doing in reallife? when i'm allowed to ask ...
C[labrys: haha, so many programmers play go, it's crazy
C[hurik: yes, a friend who is programmer too recommended it to mee
C[hurik: and what are you doing?
C[labrys: ivr phone systems for doctors. pretty basic programming, but I get to travel a bit
C[labrys: the only downside is the company language. it's only used by them, and it's about 30 years out of date
C[labrys: yeah. they only updated it a couple of years ago to handle arrays!
C[labrys: haha - those are a dream in this language!
C[labrys: half the code is gotos
hurik: can't you not implement it yourself? or do you need perfomance?
C[labrys: we have a lot of standard cod we have to use. it's slowly being replaced with more modern code. the language itself though was based on an old one called clipper
labrys: but i guess reading things out on the phone doesn't need much
C[labrys: most of what we do is databases
C[hurik: don't know what to do next
labrys: if you have no more moves, pass
labrys: 2 passes in a row ends the game I think
hurik: i was lucky in the down right corner
hurik: to get you out of there
labrys: nah. I was struggling to work out what to do with the handicap stones
labrys: couldn't think of a strategy
hurik: yes because of that too :)
labrys: you're good at capturing battles though
hurik: ok ... i'm gooing ... will chalange you tommorrow
C[labrys: Doesn't look like I can win this one. Thanks for the game :)
C[Tom Newman: Have a good game.
Tom Newman: Looks like you are new to Nova.  Welcome! Hope you like it.
C[labrys: ah - tournaments started - back later sorry
C[labrys: ok - fastest game of 9x9 ever!
C[labrys: doesn't look like I can win this one, so thanks for the game :)
C[SunPin: How are you doing? I've seen you around Reddit.
C[labrys: guilty as charged. I'm good ta. Did you say you were a programmer in chat earlier?
C[SunPin: Yes, I am. Multiple languages. Primarily Python these days.

C[labrys: Python I've not really used. Mainly I'm stuck with work's bespoke language. I'm training some new graduates in it at the moment, and watching their hopes fade as they realise how old this language is
C[SunPin: I work for a local TV station. Lots of database work, sockets (interfacing with the TV equipment), server management… Python helps me get things done quickly.
C[labrys: sounds good. I do IVR phone systems for banks and the like, so not massively complicated on the whole
SunPin: But it sounds like a nice job if you can get it. :-)
C[labrys: eh, it's a job. Keeps me busy until I find some way of making a million and retiring
C[SunPin: How is it going today?
C[labrys: not bad. working late, so getting a few sneaky games in from work
C[SunPin: Getting a a lot of nonsense out of the way. Go is a good respite.
C[labrys: how's things today? Ready for the weekend?
C[SunPin: Going well. The weekend will give me time to do some writing. Code and prose. How about you?
C[labrys: on call unfortunatly, but got an archery tournament on sunday so hoping it stays quiet
labrys: the bonus for being on call isn't nearly enough
SunPin: That's cool. Do you use those satellite looking bows they had in the London Olympics?
C[labrys: watched some of it yeah. I'm a recurve archer, so mine isn't anywhere near as futuristic as the compound bows you get
C[labrys: oddly enough, I'm from nottingham
labrys: although the club has banned green clothing and robin hood hats
labrys: bit of a change to nova this morning
SunPin: Yes, all of the sudden it's crazy around here. :-) I think it's terrific. I'm about to release speech recognition code for nova so they will get more good news.
SunPin: Sorry about not being gender-neutral with my aristocratic greetings.
C[labrys: nah, no worries. spend too much time faffing with speech recognition at work. trying to get it working with foreign languages is horrific! Def not something I'd do for fun.
C[labrys: what'll it be capable of here?

C[SunPin: It is a Windows 7 speech recognition macros set. I built it with Python as I will not ever manually place 361 points. You'll be able to call out coordinates using the NATO/military alphabet for the x-axis and the number for the y-axis. Submit, analyze, pass, resign and pause game.
C[labrys: that sounds pretty useful actually
C[labrys: I did a  bit of work  re-doing websites for blind people years ago - sites like this were impossible for screen readers to deal with
SunPin: Very useful to me. I can't type. On some days, messing with the mouse is a bit troublesome.Luckily, I'm something of a genius with computers.
C[SunPin: Pretty sure you crushed me in this game.
labrys: yeah. things have moved on so much in the last few years. new technology is great
C[labrys: pretty sure you've won actualy, but I wan't at least 1 game I haven't had to resign!
labrys: 14 straight losses on nova - painful
SunPin: Have you tried go child? I found it to be very good. No words. Just solve the problems. The concepts are broken down into digestible parts.
C[labrys: yeah, I've been working through those, but always seem to miss them in ames
labrys: we'll have to play again some time
SunPin: Do you want a review? Do you have questions?
labrys: No, I'm ok, thanks. Was reviewing mistakes as I went along. hopefully I'll spot them next time :)
SunPin: We certainly had time for that. :-) Until next time.
labrys: until next time. have a good weekend :)
Rhizy: hi gg to you too
Rhizy: hi gg to you too
C[Senffarbe: Hello, have a nice game!
C[labrys: unfortunatly I need to go to work in a few mintes, but I'll log back in later. Being on call over the weekend is rubbish, sorry

C[labrys: something tells me I am out-classed :) Thanks for the game

labrys: let me know if you fancy another game later - i'll be back after dinner
C[labrys: Thanks for the game Drbobc. Think I need to swot up a bit more :)
C[quirck: Hi! Have a good game
C[labrys: I think you've won this one. Good game, thanks :)
quirck: Thank you for the game, well played
C[hurik: i should have seen this ... damn
C[labrys: playing a game against a 10k at the moment, and being destroyed
C[hurik: don't understand your E7 stone ...
C[labrys: sorry, didn't see your comment until now
hurik: but the stones are dead ....
labrys: E7 is to try to break in to your area. I think I can save those stones
hurik: i think i can pass ... because when you really want you cann destroy all my stones ecept the upper left corner
labrys: i can do a quick review after
C[labrys: ok, i've not done a review on nova, so give me a sec to figure it out :)
C[Klangfarbe: Hi, have a nice game :)
C[Klangfarbe: Hi, have a nice game :)
C[labrys: how busy are you? did you want to try to finish this off tonight? I've got about 30 mins free. Not a problem if not :)
C[Klangfarbe: hi, oh sorry. have nit seen your message. I am playing on my S4 and due to the small screen the chat was hidden. I have time :)
C[labrys: All right, I've got a 6am start tomorrow, so I'm going to get some sleep. Have a good night :)
labrys: it really needs to beep or something :) I'm good. You?
Klangfarbe: Fine :) Sitting relaxed here on the couch
C[labrys: sounds good. I'm sneakily playing at work, but will be the end of my shift soon
C[Klangfarbe: What are you doing for living? And you are doing night shifts?
C[labrys: i'm a programmer. usually day shift, but every few months we need to cover the help desk if our systems break
labrys: irritating, but get a bonus so shouldn't complain!
C[Klangfarbe: oh, we had to do this as well for a few years. Thankfully this is over :) I am a developer as well
C[labrys: ha - seems like everyone on nova is a programmer!
C[Klangfarbe: is it so? no wonder I would say :D
C[labrys: yeah, it's a good game for geeks :)
labrys: all right, nearly home time. Will have to finish this off tomorrow I think. Have a good night
labrys: we'll have to play again some time
Klangfarbe: If you want we can do a review later. But I have to go to our Go evening now :)
labrys: yeah, would be an idea. have a good night
Klangfarbe: Ok, maybe beginning of next week? I am away on a tournament this weekend
labrys: good luck in the tournament
Klangfarbe: Thanks a lot. Have a nice evening

crodgers: i can analyze my game
crodgers: is that a live option?
C[anoek: crodgers: you wont see this until the game is over, but if analysis mode has not been disabled, yes you can do in game analysis in live games as well.

C[labrys: you're officially my most feared opponent now
integer: but i am so nice and friendly, who would fear me :(
C[labrys: a kyu player who's never played against a dan before :( It'll all be over soon :D
C[integer: you've never played a dan before?
integer: i seem to remember your nick from somewhere though
integer: or where in the UK are you from
integer: plus i'm not dan in EGF
labrys: KGS, but 11k there. I'm in Notts
C[integer: ""Nottinghamshire, abbreviated Notts""? hehe, cute name.
integer: but ok, maybe I haven't seen you before then. just seemed to remember some labr*y*-ish nick from another turn-based go server
C[integer: note that when playing N8 here you better be sure you'll win the ko at ... well analyze is disabled so I can't show variation, but white cuts at M8 then atari L9
integer: my aim with L2 is to create ko threats for that ko if I want to fight it. or just connect solidly if you veer off
integer: or if you take at N10 I'll probably play L5 or something
C[labrys: cheers :) my plan was you'd connect at n10 so I could cover the cut
C[labrys: ah, i see! I was hoping to last past 50 moves against you though :) Thanks for the game integer
C[labrys: I'm too far behind, so thanks for the game :)
Rainbow Dash: Thanks for the game :)

hurik: i don't want to tack my move back
C[labrys: No worries. I'm back in india for a couple of months for work. My company is starting an office in India's silicon valley - they call it Cyberabad - so I'm here training programmers again
C[hurik: yes, thanks but unfortunately i got this one only by accident when i was analysing the game ...
C[labrys: heh - that's half of how I play :) Will need to log off though soon - getting late here
C[hurik: ok, what time is it?
C[labrys: yeah. think it's gmt+5.5. they don't move their clocks for summer/winter, so half the time it's only gmt+4.5, which is so much easier!

C[JustForFun: Hi. Hope you enjoy the game.
JustForFun: You will probably win! Out if curiosity: why have you joined the UK group?

C[labrys: I'm English, was just working in India for a few months so it made sense to change the flag so people didn't get confused about the time zone. Flew back yesterday. When the landing was delayed for 15 minutes due to bad weather, I knew I was home
JustForFun: Ha ha! Indeed...... although its been quite mild here this December.

C[labrys: yeah, it's not so bad, but a bit of a shock after the 30-odd degrees i was used to - although that's winter temperatures, so a lot of people are wearing jumpers and coats, and looking at me like I'm insane when I'm out in just a t-shirt
